title: Coronidium glutinosum
taxonomy: Coronidium glutinosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Coronidium, which includes several species of shrubs native to Australia. Coronidium glutinosum is further classified as a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae. This classification is based on its characteristics and its relationship to other plants in the Asteraceae family. Coronidium glutinosum is considered a part of the flowering plant kingdom, Plantae.

intro: Coronidium glutinosum, also known as Australian bush daisy, is a flowering shrub native to Australia, belonging to the plant family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and promote coughing. The bioactive constituents of Coronidium glutinosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
